features.evidence: 2 in display featuring rounded corners that not only enhance the phone s ergonomics but it also provides a more comfortable grip without digging into my hand the overall design maintains the familiar glass sandwich with the staple camera bump at the back which unfortunately tends to accumulate all sorts of dirt even with a case installed build quality is excellent the buttons feel nice and tactile with no signs of rattling i ve got the obsidian black model but you can spice it up with a few other options this newly announced mint green hazel or rose i really wish that google maintain consistency in their design aesthetics throughout the pixel 8 lineup because the frosted texture on the the back of the pixel 8 pro feels much more premium compared to the glossy finish on the back which is naturally going to be a huge fingerprint magnet but then again it s nothing that a case can solve the aging optical fingerprint sensor is still in use for logging in but it tends to only work about 90 of the time one notable downside is how it can be blinding at night when trying to log into the phone personally i would have preferred an ultrasonic reader what s found in the galaxy s23 s24 series it s just known for superior accuracy and responsive but you can always set up face unlock as a backup the display has received a spec bump as well from 90 hz moled to 120 hz oland now this isn t ltpo the pixel 8 pro or samsung s s24 series but what you will notice is that it s gotten much brighter this time we re talking close to 42 improvement which is pretty wild so 2,000 nits peak versus 1400 nits on the pixel 7 and this results in exceptional visibility outdoors content looks vivid and sharp with those inky blacks that we all love so visually this is an awesome display and you d also think that the 120 hz refresh rate would add to a smooth navigating experience right well firstly when setting up the phone google for some reason sets it at 60 hz out of the box so you ll have to manually go into the settings tab and switch it to0 once that s done it s a pretty it s a pretty smooth experience with google s native apps and the os gestures are somewhat fluid but sadly that takes a turn when you venture into third party apps reddit whatsapp microsoft teams even some of my banking apps heck even google s own youtube studio just feels so jittery and i want to emphasize that part highly guys because it s super inconsistent when you re switching between apps it almost feels the display reverted itself back to 60 hz despite me forcing it to run at its peak refresh raate through the developer settings now i really wanted to investigate this disparity and i think i found the problem or the answer and no this hasn t to do with the tensor g3 chip although i will get to that in a moment but rather it s about compatibility you might be wondering well isn t this a google smartphone running google s own operating system yeah but you have to realize that android is an open source platform running on a variety of devices out there this can range from a 100 smartphone to a 2,000 smartphone and so at the end of the day it s about ensuring these popular apps run reliably on these devices worldwide running different versions of android you can t simply optimize an app for one particular android smartphone no developer would remotely go that far compared to ios for instance which has a pretty strict framework which is why apps tend to run a lot smoother on those devices and i feel this complexity alone is probably one of the main reasons why you can never expect a consistent experience on the pixel 8 and trust me this applies to the pixel 8 pro as well to add to that i had high expectations for tensor g3 on the pixel 8 and the pixel 8 pro now over the course of my testing period i still feel the performance lags behind that of even samsung or even one oneplus devices for instance the camera app still remains sluggish in capturing photos with prolonged processing times for each shot i felt i had to constantly work to keep up with the phone s performance rather than enjoying a seamless and intuitive user experience i to be creative with my photos in lightroom and pixel 8 is just not enough to keep up with my workflow efficiently google maps was also super laggy during navigation it it s just disappointing that despite the 700 and 1,000 price tags for the pixel 8 and pixel 8 pro they just aren t equipped with a flashship level processor i just feel the price doesn t justify the user experience google emphasizes that these devices are optimized for processing google ai faster and efficiently but when it comes to intensive tasks hardcore gaming they just simply don t match to qualcomm s or apple s offerings while performance might not be a top priority for the average consumer i wonder how that would get affected over a year or two with software updates i i can t help but think that maybe switching to qualcomm s s so which currently not boasts a built in npu for faster llm processing could potentially address these issues i don t know what are your thoughts on that now if we set aside performance and focus on power efficiency you re going to love the pixel 8 especially compared to the pixel 7 because they ve increased the battery size while maintaining a slimmer and more compact form factor i was consistently achieving close to 6 hours of screen on time which is way better than my iphone 15 pro now granted battery usage can vary from one user to the other but for me on my typical work days where i m at the studio glued to my computer i managed to go nearly two days without needing to recharge so there s one good thing that came out of tensor g3 chip it s the positive impact on battery life which is great news for anyone looking for a small phone the charging speeds have also improved thanks to the increased power delivery you also still get wireless charging and if me i ve always appreciated mac safe on iphone so i did miss that part on the pixel 8 when i switched to this phone but you can easily get a compatible case to address that so i m here at the canadian international auto show super excited about this for february cuz it s something exciting for the winter here in canada and better way to test the cameras on the pixel 8 so we re going to go shoot some cars and test out these amazing cameras cuz they re pretty good so the front facing camera is a 10

2 in display featuring rounded corners that not only enhance the phone s ergonomics but it also provides a more comfortable grip without digging into my hand the overall design maintains the familiar glass sandwich with the staple camera bump at the back which unfortunately tends to accumulate all sorts of dirt even with a case installed build quality is excellent the buttons feel nice and tactile with no signs of rattling i ve got the obsidian black model but you can spice it up with a few other options this newly announced mint green hazel or rose i really wish that google maintain consistency in their design aesthetics throughout the pixel 8 lineup because the frosted texture on the the back of the pixel 8 pro feels much more premium compared to the glossy finish on the back which is naturally going to be a huge fingerprint magnet but then again it s nothing that a case can solve the aging optical fingerprint sensor is still in use for logging in but it tends to only work about 90 of the time one notable downside is how it can be blinding at night when trying to log into the phone personally i would have preferred an ultrasonic reader what s found in the galaxy s23 s24 series it s just known for superior accuracy and responsive but you can always set up face unlock as a backup the display has received a spec bump as well from 90 hz moled to 120 hz oland now this isn t ltpo the pixel 8 pro or samsung s s24 series but what you will notice is that it s gotten much brighter this time we re talking close to 42 improvement which is pretty wild so 2,000 nits peak versus 1400 nits on the pixel 7 and this results in exceptional visibility outdoors content looks vivid and sharp with those inky blacks that we all love so visually this is an awesome display and you d also think that the 120 hz refresh rate would add to a smooth navigating experience right well firstly when setting up the phone google for some reason sets it at 60 hz out of the box so you ll have to manually go into the settings tab and switch it to0 once that s done it s a pretty it s a pretty smooth experience with google s native apps and the os gestures are somewhat fluid but sadly that takes a turn when you venture into third party apps reddit whatsapp microsoft teams even some of my banking apps heck even google s own youtube studio just feels so jittery and i want to emphasize that part highly guys because it s super inconsistent when you re switching between apps it almost feels the display reverted itself back to 60 hz despite me forcing it to run at its peak refresh raate through the developer settings now i really wanted to investigate this disparity and i think i found the problem or the answer and no this hasn t to do with the tensor g3 chip although i will get to that in a moment but rather it s about compatibility you might be wondering well isn t this a google smartphone running google s own operating system yeah but you have to realize that android is an open source platform running on a variety of devices out there this can range from a 100 smartphone to a 2,000 smartphone and so at the end of the day it s about ensuring these popular apps run reliably on these devices worldwide running different versions of android you can t simply optimize an app for one particular android smartphone no developer would remotely go that far compared to ios for instance which has a pretty strict framework which is why apps tend to run a lot smoother on those devices and i feel this complexity alone is probably one of the main reasons why you can never expect a consistent experience on the pixel 8 and trust me this applies to the pixel 8 pro as well to add to that i had high expectations for tensor g3 on the pixel 8 and the pixel 8 pro now over the course of my testing period i still feel the performance lags behind that of even samsung or even one oneplus devices for instance the camera app still remains sluggish in capturing photos with prolonged processing times for each shot i felt i had to constantly work to keep up with the phone s performance rather than enjoying a seamless and intuitive user experience i to be creative with my photos in lightroom and pixel 8 is just not enough to keep up with my workflow efficiently google maps was also super laggy during navigation it it s just disappointing that despite the 700 and 1,000 price tags for the pixel 8 and pixel 8 pro they just aren t equipped with a flashship level processor i just feel the price doesn t justify the user experience google emphasizes that these devices are optimized for processing google ai faster and efficiently but when it comes to intensive tasks hardcore gaming they just simply don t match to qualcomm s or apple s offerings while performance might not be a top priority for the average consumer i wonder how that would get affected over a year or two with software updates i i can t help but think that maybe switching to qualcomm s s so which currently not boasts a built in npu for faster llm processing could potentially address these issues i don t know what are your thoughts on that now if we set aside performance and focus on power efficiency you re going to love the pixel 8 especially compared to the pixel 7 because they ve increased the battery size while maintaining a slimmer and more compact form factor i was consistently achieving close to 6 hours of screen on time which is way better than my iphone 15 pro now granted battery usage can vary from one user to the other but for me on my typical work days where i m at the studio glued to my computer i managed to go nearly two days without needing to recharge so there s one good thing that came out of tensor g3 chip it s the positive impact on battery life which is great news for anyone looking for a small phone the charging speeds have also improved thanks to the increased power delivery you also still get wireless charging and if me i ve always appreciated mac safe on iphone so i did miss that part on the pixel 8 when i switched to this phone but you can easily get a compatible case to address that so i m here at the canadian international auto show super excited about this for february cuz it s something exciting for the winter here in canada and better way to test the cameras on the pixel 8 so we re going to go shoot some cars and test out these amazing cameras cuz they re pretty good so the front facing camera is a 10 However, in this video, i want to break down my experience of using it, covering what i and some of the problems i ve encountered

2 in display featuring rounded corners that not only enhance the phone s ergonomics but it also provides a more comfortable grip without digging into my hand the overall design maintains the familiar glass sandwich with the staple camera bump at the back which unfortunately tends to accumulate all sorts of dirt even with a case installed build quality is excellent the buttons feel nice and tactile with no signs of rattling i ve got the obsidian black model but you can spice it up with a few other options this newly announced mint green hazel or rose i really wish that google maintain consistency in their design aesthetics throughout the pixel 8 lineup because the frosted texture on the the back of the pixel 8 pro feels much more premium compared to the glossy finish on the back which is naturally going to be a huge fingerprint magnet but then again it s nothing that a case can solve the aging optical fingerprint sensor is still in use for logging in but it tends to only work about 90 of the time one notable downside is how it can be blinding at night when trying to log into the phone personally i would have preferred an ultrasonic reader what s found in the galaxy s23 s24 series it s just known for superior accuracy and responsive but you can always set up face unlock as a backup the display has received a spec bump as well from 90 hz moled to 120 hz oland now this isn t ltpo the pixel 8 pro or samsung s s24 series but what you will notice is that it s gotten much brighter this time we re talking close to 42 improvement which is pretty wild so 2,000 nits peak versus 1400 nits on the pixel 7 and this results in exceptional visibility outdoors content looks vivid and sharp with those inky blacks that we all love so visually this is an awesome display and you d also think that the 120 hz refresh rate would add to a smooth navigating experience right well firstly when setting up the phone google for some reason sets it at 60 hz out of the box so you ll have to manually go into the settings tab and switch it to0 once that s done it s a pretty it s a pretty smooth experience with google s native apps and the os gestures are somewhat fluid but sadly that takes a turn when you venture into third party apps reddit whatsapp microsoft teams even some of my banking apps heck even google s own youtube studio just feels so jittery and i want to emphasize that part highly guys because it s super inconsistent when you re switching between apps it almost feels the display reverted itself back to 60 hz despite me forcing it to run at its peak refresh raate through the developer settings now i really wanted to investigate this disparity and i think i found the problem or the answer and no this hasn t to do with the tensor g3 chip although i will get to that in a moment but rather it s about compatibility you might be wondering well isn t this a google smartphone running google s own operating system yeah but you have to realize that android is an open source platform running on a variety of devices out there this can range from a 100 smartphone to a 2,000 smartphone and so at the end of the day it s about ensuring these popular apps run reliably on these devices worldwide running different versions of android you can t simply optimize an app for one particular android smartphone no developer would remotely go that far compared to ios for instance which has a pretty strict framework which is why apps tend to run a lot smoother on those devices and i feel this complexity alone is probably one of the main reasons why you can never expect a consistent experience on the pixel 8 and trust me this applies to the pixel 8 pro as well to add to that i had high expectations for tensor g3 on the pixel 8 and the pixel 8 pro now over the course of my testing period i still feel the performance lags behind that of even samsung or even one oneplus devices for instance the camera app still remains sluggish in capturing photos with prolonged processing times for each shot i felt i had to constantly work to keep up with the phone s performance rather than enjoying a seamless and intuitive user experience i to be creative with my photos in lightroom and pixel 8 is just not enough to keep up with my workflow efficiently google maps was also super laggy during navigation it it s just disappointing that despite the 700 and 1,000 price tags for the pixel 8 and pixel 8 pro they just aren t equipped with a flashship level processor i just feel the price doesn t justify the user experience google emphasizes that these devices are optimized for processing google ai faster and efficiently but when it comes to intensive tasks hardcore gaming they just simply don t match to qualcomm s or apple s offerings while performance might not be a top priority for the average consumer i wonder how that would get affected over a year or two with software updates i i can t help but think that maybe switching to qualcomm s s so which currently not boasts a built in npu for faster llm processing could potentially address these issues i don t know what are your thoughts on that now if we set aside performance and focus on power efficiency you re going to love the pixel 8 especially compared to the pixel 7 because they ve increased the battery size while maintaining a slimmer and more compact form factor i was consistently achieving close to 6 hours of screen on time which is way better than my iphone 15 pro now granted battery usage can vary from one user to the other but for me on my typical work days where i m at the studio glued to my computer i managed to go nearly two days without needing to recharge so there s one good thing that came out of tensor g3 chip it s the positive impact on battery life which is great news for anyone looking for a small phone the charging speeds have also improved thanks to the increased power delivery you also still get wireless charging and if me i ve always appreciated mac safe on iphone so i did miss that part on the pixel 8 when i switched to this phone but you can easily get a compatible case to address that so i m here at the canadian international auto show super excited about this for february cuz it s something exciting for the winter here in canada and better way to test the cameras on the pixel 8 so we re going to go shoot some cars and test out these amazing cameras cuz they re pretty good so the front facing camera is a 10

so here s the thing i m a sucker for small ph factor phones they re convenient to carry easy for oneand use and i adore their discret profile in my pocket so when google dropped the pixel 8 about 4 months ago i was immediately drawn to its size and some of its software wizardry thanks to ai of course you see i ve always been a fan of google phones in fact my foray into android smartphones was with the google play edition htc1 not sure if you guys remember that but i spe specifically chosea because it had top notch hardware back then and it ran the purest form of android i didn t prefer any of those skins running on top of the open source platform so over time google recognized the need to establish its presence and compete with the big players leading to the inception of the pixel series and it s been eight years since introduction of the first pixel so the question remains has google succeeded or fallen short i m hoping to address that by closely examining the pixel 8 under the microscope after the updates all right so i m going to quickly start by going over some of the minor hardware improvements that the pixel 8 brings to the table compared to the pixel 7 a notable change is the smaller 6

2 in oled with a 1080p resolution a 120 hz refresh rate and gorilla glass f dis protection this 120 hz is a nice step up from the 90 hz on the pixel 7 swiping and scrolling look plenty smooth and the screen will dial back down to 60 hz when idling to save energy i mentioned that the max brightness has improved this year and it s really quite impressive we were able to achieve over 1,000 nits maximum with the manual slider and this could boost to over 1,400 nits in auto mode when exposed to bright light that makes the pixel 8 one of the brightest phones around excellent for use outdoors the display looks good too it s sharp the contrast is great and the colors can be quite accurate the pixel 8 also has support for hdr10 plus video for audio there s a stereo speaker setup here the loudness is very good and the sound quality is good too with enough base and clear vocals i ll drop a link so you can check it out for yourself the pixel 8 has an under display optical fingerprint reader and is quite reliable and when it comes to storage you can get the phone with 1208 or 256 gigs on board that isn t expandable via micro sd only with the google pixels will you get the pure android experience exactly as google intended and the pixel 8 also has the newest android version out of the box before anyone else android 14 cosmetically is the same as the previous version there are a few new features worth mentioning though for example the lock screen is much more customizable you can also create your own wallpapers with plenty of main styles to work with and prompt combinations of words and colors android 14 also gives you the option to choose a regional language but use a different measurement system and date format and there s a new privacy feature when sending images to someone that app won t have access to your whole gallery before android 14 also supports lossless audio with wired headphones and 10bit hdr images onto the pixel specific ai based features live translate is even faster now it s interpreter mode can translate a conversation in real time if that language is supported the call screening feature where the google assistant can take a call for you or transcribe rooc call options is also better and faster this year thanks to the pixels ai you can easily extract text from apps or even images finally there s a new more powerful magic editor within the photos app it offers advanced functions removing objects from the photo and changing the sky and last but definitely not least google promises both os and security updates for the next 7 years on the new pixels that s more than any other smartphone maker at the heart of all of these features is google s tensor g3 chipset based on a 4 nanometer process it brings both a new gpu for improved graphics performance and google s titan m2 chip which handles the ai related tasks with this new chipset the pixel 8 is clearly more powerful than its predecessor both when it comes to the cpu and the graphics however still falls way behind current flagship competitors running on a snapdragon hn2 as far as the raw numbers go it s more on par with last year s flagships google stresses that it tries to optimize its devices instead of ramping up performance and the pixel 8 does do a great job in day to day tasks still this may not be the best choice for heavy gamers this is further reinforced by our results when it comes to thermal management in our prolonged stress test the pixel 8 displayed significant thermal throttling which included some sharp dips the pixel 8 has a larger battery capacity than the previous model 4575 mah versus 4,355 even so the battery life hasn t improved rather the endurance rating is less this year with the pixel 8 earning an overall score of 83 hours however if you just look at the numbers for screen on time the results are about the same as last year charging has been improved from 20 w to 27 and the charging is a bit faster but still nothing special with the pixel 8 able to top up from 0 to 56 in half an hour there s also support for wireless charging now let s get into the pixel 8 s cameras on the back there s a 50 map main cam and a 12 map ultra wide cam the main camera uses the same sensor as last year but has a brighter f1

2 in display featuring rounded corners that not only enhance the phone s ergonomics but it also provides a more comfortable grip without digging into my hand the overall design maintains the familiar glass sandwich with the staple camera bump at the back which unfortunately tends to accumulate all sorts of dirt even with a case installed build quality is excellent the buttons feel nice and tactile with no signs of rattling i ve got the obsidian black model but you can spice it up with a few other options this newly announced mint green hazel or rose i really wish that google maintain consistency in their design aesthetics throughout the pixel 8 lineup because the frosted texture on the the back of the pixel 8 pro feels much more premium compared to the glossy finish on the back which is naturally going to be a huge fingerprint magnet but then again it s nothing that a case can solve the aging optical fingerprint sensor is still in use for logging in but it tends to only work about 90 of the time one notable downside is how it can be blinding at night when trying to log into the phone personally i would have preferred an ultrasonic reader what s found in the galaxy s23 s24 series it s just known for superior accuracy and responsive but you can always set up face unlock as a backup the display has received a spec bump as well from 90 hz moled to 120 hz oland now this isn t ltpo the pixel 8 pro or samsung s s24 series but what you will notice is that it s gotten much brighter this time we re talking close to 42 improvement which is pretty wild so 2,000 nits peak versus 1400 nits on the pixel 7 and this results in exceptional visibility outdoors content looks vivid and sharp with those inky blacks that we all love so visually this is an awesome display and you d also think that the 120 hz refresh rate would add to a smooth navigating experience right well firstly when setting up the phone google for some reason sets it at 60 hz out of the box so you ll have to manually go into the settings tab and switch it to0 once that s done it s a pretty it s a pretty smooth experience with google s native apps and the os gestures are somewhat fluid but sadly that takes a turn when you venture into third party apps reddit whatsapp microsoft teams even some of my banking apps heck even google s own youtube studio just feels so jittery and i want to emphasize that part highly guys because it s super inconsistent when you re switching between apps it almost feels the display reverted itself back to 60 hz despite me forcing it to run at its peak refresh raate through the developer settings now i really wanted to investigate this disparity and i think i found the problem or the answer and no this hasn t to do with the tensor g3 chip although i will get to that in a moment but rather it s about compatibility you might be wondering well isn t this a google smartphone running google s own operating system yeah but you have to realize that android is an open source platform running on a variety of devices out there this can range from a 100 smartphone to a 2,000 smartphone and so at the end of the day it s about ensuring these popular apps run reliably on these devices worldwide running different versions of android you can t simply optimize an app for one particular android smartphone no developer would remotely go that far compared to ios for instance which has a pretty strict framework which is why apps tend to run a lot smoother on those devices and i feel this complexity alone is probably one of the main reasons why you can never expect a consistent experience on the pixel 8 and trust me this applies to the pixel 8 pro as well to add to that i had high expectations for tensor g3 on the pixel 8 and the pixel 8 pro now over the course of my testing period i still feel the performance lags behind that of even samsung or even one oneplus devices for instance the camera app still remains sluggish in capturing photos with prolonged processing times for each shot i felt i had to constantly work to keep up with the phone s performance rather than enjoying a seamless and intuitive user experience i to be creative with my photos in lightroom and pixel 8 is just not enough to keep up with my workflow efficiently google maps was also super laggy during navigation it it s just disappointing that despite the 700 and 1,000 price tags for the pixel 8 and pixel 8 pro they just aren t equipped with a flashship level processor i just feel the price doesn t justify the user experience google emphasizes that these devices are optimized for processing google ai faster and efficiently but when it comes to intensive tasks hardcore gaming they just simply don t match to qualcomm s or apple s offerings while performance might not be a top priority for the average consumer i wonder how that would get affected over a year or two with software updates i i can t help but think that maybe switching to qualcomm s s so which currently not boasts a built in npu for faster llm processing could potentially address these issues i don t know what are your thoughts on that now if we set aside performance and focus on power efficiency you re going to love the pixel 8 especially compared to the pixel 7 because they ve increased the battery size while maintaining a slimmer and more compact form factor i was consistently achieving close to 6 hours of screen on time which is way better than my iphone 15 pro now granted battery usage can vary from one user to the other but for me on my typical work days where i m at the studio glued to my computer i managed to go nearly two days without needing to recharge so there s one good thing that came out of tensor g3 chip it s the positive impact on battery life which is great news for anyone looking for a small phone the charging speeds have also improved thanks to the increased power delivery you also still get wireless charging and if me i ve always appreciated mac safe on iphone so i did miss that part on the pixel 8 when i switched to this phone but you can easily get a compatible case to address that so i m here at the canadian international auto show super excited about this for february cuz it s something exciting for the winter here in canada and better way to test the cameras on the pixel 8 so we re going to go shoot some cars and test out these amazing cameras cuz they re pretty good so the front facing camera is a 10

review_summary: 2 in display featuring rounded corners that not only enhance the phone s ergonomics but it also provides a more comfortable grip without digging into my hand the overall design maintains the familiar glass sandwich with the staple camera bump at the back which unfortunately tends to accumulate all sorts of dirt even with a case installed build quality is excellent the buttons feel nice and tactile with no signs of rattling i ve got the obsidian black model but you can spice it up with a few other options this newly announced mint green hazel or rose i really wish that google maintain consistency in their design aesthetics throughout the pixel 8 lineup because the frosted texture on the the back of the pixel 8 pro feels much more premium compared to the glossy finish on the back which is naturally going to be a huge fingerprint magnet but then again it s nothing that a case can solve the aging optical fingerprint sensor is still in use for logging in but it tends to only work about 90 of the time one notable downside is how it can be blinding at night when trying to log into the phone personally i would have preferred an ultrasonic reader what s found in the galaxy s23 s24 series it s just known for superior accuracy and responsive but you can always set up face unlock as a backup the display has received a spec bump as well from 90 hz moled to 120 hz oland now this isn t ltpo the pixel 8 pro or samsung s s24 series but what you will notice is that it s gotten much brighter this time we re talking close to 42 improvement which is pretty wild so 2,000 nits peak versus 1400 nits on the pixel 7 and this results in exceptional visibility outdoors content looks vivid and sharp with those inky blacks that we all love so visually this is an awesome display and you d also think that the 120 hz refresh rate would add to a smooth navigating experience right well firstly when setting up the phone google for some reason sets it at 60 hz out of the box so you ll have to manually go into the settings tab and switch it to0 once that s done it s a pretty it s a pretty smooth experience with google s native apps and the os gestures are somewhat fluid but sadly that takes a turn when you venture into third party apps reddit whatsapp microsoft teams even some of my banking apps heck even google s own youtube studio just feels so jittery and i want to emphasize that part highly guys because it s super inconsistent when you re switching between apps it almost feels the display reverted itself back to 60 hz despite me forcing it to run at its peak refresh raate through the developer settings now i really wanted to investigate this disparity and i think i found the problem or the answer and no this hasn t to do with the tensor g3 chip although i will get to that in a moment but rather it s about compatibility you might be wondering well isn t this a google smartphone running google s own operating system yeah but you have to realize that android is an open source platform running on a variety of devices out there this can range from a 100 smartphone to a 2,000 smartphone and so at the end of the day it s about ensuring these popular apps run reliably on these devices worldwide running different versions of android you can t simply optimize an app for one particular android smartphone no developer would remotely go that far compared to ios for instance which has a pretty strict framework which is why apps tend to run a lot smoother on those devices and i feel this complexity alone is probably one of the main reasons why you can never expect a consistent experience on the pixel 8 and trust me this applies to the pixel 8 pro as well to add to that i had high expectations for tensor g3 on the pixel 8 and the pixel 8 pro now over the course of my testing period i still feel the performance lags behind that of even samsung or even one oneplus devices for instance the camera app still remains sluggish in capturing photos with prolonged processing times for each shot i felt i had to constantly work to keep up with the phone s performance rather than enjoying a seamless and intuitive user experience i to be creative with my photos in lightroom and pixel 8 is just not enough to keep up with my workflow efficiently google maps was also super laggy during navigation it it s just disappointing that despite the 700 and 1,000 price tags for the pixel 8 and pixel 8 pro they just aren t equipped with a flashship level processor i just feel the price doesn t justify the user experience google emphasizes that these devices are optimized for processing google ai faster and efficiently but when it comes to intensive tasks hardcore gaming they just simply don t match to qualcomm s or apple s offerings while performance might not be a top priority for the average consumer i wonder how that would get affected over a year or two with software updates i i can t help but think that maybe switching to qualcomm s s so which currently not boasts a built in npu for faster llm processing could potentially address these issues i don t know what are your thoughts on that now if we set aside performance and focus on power efficiency you re going to love the pixel 8 especially compared to the pixel 7 because they ve increased the battery size while maintaining a slimmer and more compact form factor i was consistently achieving close to 6 hours of screen on time which is way better than my iphone 15 pro now granted battery usage can vary from one user to the other but for me on my typical work days where i m at the studio glued to my computer i managed to go nearly two days without needing to recharge so there s one good thing that came out of tensor g3 chip it s the positive impact on battery life which is great news for anyone looking for a small phone the charging speeds have also improved thanks to the increased power delivery you also still get wireless charging and if me i ve always appreciated mac safe on iphone so i did miss that part on the pixel 8 when i switched to this phone but you can easily get a compatible case to address that so i m here at the canadian international auto show super excited about this for february cuz it s something exciting for the winter here in canada and better way to test the cameras on the pixel 8 so we re going to go well firstly when setting up the phone google for some reason sets it at 60 hz out of the box so you ll have to manually go into the settings tab and switch it to0 once that s done it s a pretty it s a pretty smooth experience with google s native apps and the os gestures are somewhat fluid but sadly that takes a turn when you venture into third party apps reddit whatsapp microsoft teams even some of my banking apps heck even google s own youtube studio just feels so jittery and i want to emphasize that part highly guys because it s super inconsistent when you re switching between apps it almost feels the display reverted itself back to 60 hz despite me forcing it to run at its peak refresh raate through the developer settings now i really wanted to investigate this disparity and i think i found the problem or the answer and no this hasn t to do with the tensor g3 chip although i will get to that in a moment but rather it s about compatibility you might be wondering well isn t this a google smartphone running google s own operating system yeah but you have to realize that android is an open source platform running on a variety of devices out there this can range from a 100 smartphone to a 2,000 smartphone and so at the end of the day it s about ensuring these popular apps run reliably on these devices worldwide running different versions of android you can t simply optimize an app for one particular android smartphone no developer would remotely go that far compared to ios for instance which has a pretty strict framework which is why apps tend to run a lot smoother on those devices and i feel this complexity alone is probably one of the main reasons why you can never expect a consistent experience on the pixel 8 and trust me this applies to the pixel 8 pro as well to add to that i had high expectations for tensor g3 on the pixel 8 and the pixel 8 pro now over the course of my testing period i still feel the performance lags behind that of even samsung or even one oneplus devices for instance the camera app still remains sluggish in capturing photos with prolonged processing times for each shot i felt i had to constantly work to keep up with the phone s performance rather than enjoying a seamless and intuitive user experience i to be creative with my photos in lightroom and pixel 8 is just not enough to keep up with my workflow efficiently google maps was also super laggy during navigation it it s just disappointing that despite the 700 and 1,000 price tags for the pixel 8 and pixel 8 pro they just aren t equipped with a flashship level processor i just feel the price doesn t justify the user experience google emphasizes that these devices are optimized for processing google ai faster and efficiently but when it comes to intensive tasks hardcore gaming they just simply don t match to qualcomm s or apple s offerings while performance might not be a top priority for the average consumer i wonder how that would get affected over a year or two with software updates i i can t help but think that maybe switching to qualcomm s s so which currently not boasts a built in npu for faster llm processing could potentially
